channel.title: Design on Leyline — Demo
channel.description: Recent content in Design on Leyline — Demo

item.title: Welcome to Leyline
item.description:
Leyline is a minimal Hugo theme aimed at writing. It does a few things well:

  • Sticky header with a logo, menu, and quick controls.
  • A light/dark switcher that respects your OS preference until you choose otherwise.
  • A quiet home page that surfaces featured posts (or your latest, if you haven’t picked any).

Why “Leyline”?

The name nods to invisible threads of structure — the kind of design you don’t notice because everything is where you’d expect.

item.title: Notes on typography
item.description:
A few short notes on the type choices in this theme.

One family, three weights

Leyline ships with Inter at weights 400, 500, and 700. That’s enough to support body text, UI accents, and headings without pulling in a second family.

Letter-spacing

Headings use a slight negative letter-spacing (-0.015em) to tighten the look at large sizes. Below ~1.25rem the effect is barely noticeable; above it, copy feels less airy.

Reading width

Prose containers cap at 720px — about 70 characters per line at the default size. Wider feels like a wiki; narrower feels like a Twitter thread.

item.title: Dark mode, quick take
item.description:
The theme switcher in Leyline follows a simple rule: on the first visit, match the user’s OS preference. Once they tap the toggle, lock to their choice via localStorage.

This avoids two common annoyances: a site that ignores prefers-color-scheme, and a site that resets to its default every time you come back.
